hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: You raised before the cards were dealt. Your opponent called.
Flop — 5, 9, 8. You bet. Your opponent raised you back.
Turn — Jack. Your opponent bet out. You called.

wrongReasons: 6 8 is close, but the river lead is the tell. The correct hand picked up more on the river than 6 8 would have.
10 Q makes a straight here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
6 J has little reason to lead the river here — two pair can't comfortably go for value after calling two streets.
hand.explanation:
Answer: Q 8

Your opponent flopped three of a kind and raised the flop immediately to build the pot. Sets are too strong to slow-play — they pushed their advantage on every street.
